# sim.simulate_admixture_impure_refs { #tspaint.sim.simulate_admixture_impure_refs }

```python
tspaint.sim.simulate_admixture_impure_refs(
    n_admix=10,
    n_pure=6,
    n_impure=6,
    sequence_length=2000000.0,
    recombination_rate=1e-08,
    ploidy=2,
    random_seed=42,
    mutation_rate=None,
    ref_impurity=0.1,
    **demography_kwargs,
)
```

Simulate admixed queries, a pure reference anchor core, and impure reference panels.

Samples ``n_admix`` admixed individuals (queries), ``n_pure`` from each pure source
(A, B — the trusted hard-clamp anchor core, CLAUDE.md §6) and ``n_impure`` from each
impure reference panel (``REF_A_IMPURE``, ``REF_B_IMPURE`` — nominally A / B but
carrying a ``ref_impurity`` minority of foreign tracts). The census truth covers
every sample, so the impure references' own foreign tracts are known ground truth.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**n_admix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of admixed (query) individuals.

<code>[**n_pure**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [6]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of individuals sampled from each pure source (A and B).

<code>[**n_impure**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [6]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of individuals sampled from each impure reference panel.

<code>[**sequence_length**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Sequence length in base pairs.

<code>[**recombination_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1e-08]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Per-base, per-generation recombination rate.

<code>[**ploidy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Ploidy; each individual yields ``ploidy`` sample haplotypes.

<code>[**random_seed**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [42]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Seed for :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**mutation_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If given, overlay mutations on the ARG at this per-base rate so the returned ``.ts`` carries sites (:func:`tspaint.io.add_mutations`); ``None`` (default) leaves the bare ancestry. As for :func:`simulate_admixture`.

<code>[**ref_impurity**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.1]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Minority foreign fraction of each impure reference panel (see :func:`admixture_demography_impure_refs`).

<code>[****demography_kwargs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [{}]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`admixture_demography_impure_refs` (e.g. ``T_admix``, ``T_split``, ``f_A``, ``Ne``).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Simulation](`tspaint.sim.Simulation`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A :class:`Simulation` (use ``.ts`` for the tree sequence); its sample nodes are haplotypes from the admixed, pure-source and impure-reference populations — identify them by node population (as the experiments do) rather than by index.

## See Also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also}

admixture_demography_impure_refs : The underlying demography.
local_ancestry_truth : Recover the ground-truth ancestry tracts (references included).
